raw_output_payload.reasoning: Need tar of subdirectories of /testbed, but without prefix "testbed". So use -C /testbed and tar -cf /backup1.tar .? But only subdirectories, not files. Use find. tar -cf /backup1.tar -C /testbed $(find . -maxdepth 1 -type d ! -name .) . That includes directories. Use --transform to strip prefix? Actually want archive without prefix "testbed". If we tar from /testbed, paths inside will be like subdir/... So no prefix. So command: tar -cf /backup1.tar -C /testbed $(find . -maxdepth 1 -type d ! -name .)

If we tar from /testbed, paths inside will be like subdir/... So no prefix. So command: tar -cf /backup1.tar -C /testbed $(find . -maxdepth 1 -type d ! -name .)
